summary: Matched most of Dolphin's ARAM AR module in C, leaving __ARChecksize in assembly. The PR moved ARRegisterDMACallback, ARGetDMAStatus, ARStartDMA, ARAlloc, ARFree, ARCheckInit, ARInit, ARGetBaseAddress, ARGetSize, and __ARHandler from asm/dolphin/ar/ar.s into new src/dolphin/ar/ar.c, added ar.c.o to obj_files.mk before the remaining ar.s.o, and corrected ARRegisterDMACallback's header prototype to return the previous Event callback.
what_changed: Added src/dolphin/ar/ar.c with C implementations for AR callback registration, DMA status/start, ARAM stack allocation/free, init checks, initialization, base/size getters, and the AR interrupt handler.
Removed the matched AR functions and sdata2 float/double constants from asm/dolphin/ar/ar.s while retaining __ARChecksize in assembly, consistent with the PR title's exception.
Added $(BUILD_DIR)/src/dolphin/ar/ar.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES immediately before $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/dolphin/ar/ar.s.o so the newly matched C object links alongside the remaining assembly object.
Changed src/dolphin/ar/ar.h so ARRegisterDMACallback returns Event instead of void, matching the implementation and observed assembly behavior of returning the old callback.
Implemented ARStartDMA through halfword DSP register writes using __DSPRegs indices 16-21 and explicit masking/or-ing for address, length, and transfer type fields.
Implemented ARInit setup of __AR_Callback, DSP ARAM interrupt handler/unmasking, stack pointer/free block bookkeeping, refresh register calculation, __ARChecksize call, and __AR_init_flag setting.
smart_moves: Kept __ARChecksize in asm rather than forcing an incomplete match, allowing the rest of the AR module to transition to C cleanly.

Preserved interrupt safety around shared AR state and hardware register updates by mirroring OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts patterns in ARRegisterDMACallback, ARGetDMAStatus, ARStartDMA, ARAlloc, ARFree, and ARInit.

Used extern declarations for existing small-data globals such as __AR_Callback, __AR_Size, __AR_StackPointer, __AR_FreeBlocks, __AR_BlockLength, and __AR_init_flag, avoiding unnecessary data movement while only decompiling text.
Matched the old assembly behavior of ARRegisterDMACallback by saving and returning the previous callback, then fixed the public header to expose that return type.
Separated hardware register bitfield updates into source-level masks that correspond closely to the rlwinm/rlwimi/or halfword operations in the assembly excerpt.
Handled the interrupt callback through an OSContext stack object, OSClearContext, OSSetCurrentContext, callback null-check, and restoration of the previous context, matching the assembly structure.
decomp_lessons: When converting a partially matched library file, leave hard functions in asm and add the C object before the asm object in obj_files.mk so remaining assembly symbols can continue to provide unresolved functions.
Header prototypes should be checked against the matched assembly, not just existing declarations; ARRegisterDMACallback was declared void but the assembly returned the old callback in r3.

Volatile or global hardware state often requires exact interrupt disable/restore ordering for both correctness and matching; keep the saved interrupt state variable and restore after state/register writes.
DSP MMIO register accesses can be expressed through shared register arrays like __DSPRegs when that reproduces the same halfword load/store and masking patterns.
For init routines, preserve early-return behavior before disabling interrupts; ARInit returns 0x4000 immediately when __AR_init_flag is already true.

If a floating hardware timing expression matches but the ideal SDK macro is unknown, a source-level TODO is acceptable evidence of uncertainty; this PR used `196.0F * (__OSBusClock / 202500000.0F)` with a note that an OS_TIMER_CLOCK macro should probably be used.
naming_conventions: Dolphin AR public APIs use AR-prefixed names: ARRegisterDMACallback, ARGetDMAStatus, ARStartDMA, ARAlloc, ARFree, ARCheckInit, ARInit, ARGetBaseAddress, ARGetSize.
Internal AR globals and handlers use double-underscore names: __AR_Callback, __AR_Size, __AR_StackPointer, __AR_FreeBlocks, __AR_BlockLength, __AR_init_flag, __ARHandler, __ARChecksize.
Callback type is represented as Event, and callback variables use names like callback and oldCb.
ARStartDMA parameters were named by role: type, mainmem_addr, aram_addr, length.
ARInit parameters were named stack_index_addr and num_entries, reflecting the AR allocation stack bookkeeping.
OS interrupt/context names follow Dolphin OS types and constants: __OSInterrupt, OSContext, OS_INTR_DSP_ARAM, OS_INTRMASK_DSP_ARAM.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced hand-written/remaining assembly with C only for functions that matched; __ARChecksize stayed in asm/dolphin/ar/ar.s.
Used explicit casts to u16 on __DSPRegs assignments to influence halfword codegen and match register writes.
Used masks such as ~0x3ff, ~0xffe0, ~0x8000, and `(type << 15)` to model the original rlwinm/rlwimi bit manipulation in ARStartDMA.

For ARInit's refresh calculation, used float constants 196.0F and 202500000.0F and an int temporary to reproduce the assembly's floating conversion path; removed corresponding sdata2 constants from asm once generated by C.

Matched ARFree's stack behavior by pre-decrementing __AR_BlockLength, optionally storing the freed length, subtracting `*__AR_BlockLength` from __AR_StackPointer, incrementing __AR_FreeBlocks, restoring interrupts, and returning the new stack pointer.
Matched __ARHandler's large stack OSContext behavior by declaring a local OSContext exceptionContext and bracketing the callback with OSClearContext/OSSetCurrentContext calls.
Used `static void __ARHandler(...)` in the C file while forward declaring it before ARInit, keeping it file-local yet usable for __OSSetInterruptHandler.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that match __ARChecksize and remove the remaining asm/dolphin/ar/ar.s dependency.
Check whether later work replaces ARInit's hard-coded refresh calculation with an OS_TIMER_CLOCK macro or SDK-equivalent expression.
Search for follow-up header expansions for src/dolphin/ar/ar.h, since this PR only corrected ARRegisterDMACallback and did not add prototypes for every implemented AR API.
Compare other Dolphin hardware modules for the same __DSPRegs bitfield masking style used in ARStartDMA.
